title: The Four-Day Workweek Experiment
subtitle: 每周工作四天真的可行吗

paragraphs.en: Companies worldwide are experimenting with four-day workweeks, challenging assumptions that have governed employment for over a century. These trials typically maintain full salaries while reducing hours, testing whether productivity can remain constant with better work-life balance. Early results have been remarkably positive, sparking broader interest in restructuring work schedules.
paragraphs.zh: 世界各地的公司正在试验四天工作周，挑战了一个多世纪以来主导就业的假设。这些试验通常保持全额工资同时减少工时，测试在更好的工作生活平衡下生产力是否能保持不变。早期结果非常积极，引发了对重组工作时间表的更广泛兴趣。

paragraphs.en: Studies from pilot programs reveal surprising outcomes. Rather than declining, productivity often increases as employees eliminate time-wasting activities and focus more intensely during work hours. Companies report improved recruitment and retention, as the shortened week becomes a powerful competitive advantage in attracting talent.
paragraphs.zh: 试点项目的研究揭示了令人惊讶的结果。生产力不但没有下降，反而经常提高，因为员工消除了浪费时间的活动，在工作时间更加专注。公司报告招聘和留任情况改善，因为缩短的工作周成为吸引人才的强大竞争优势。

paragraphs.en: Employee wellbeing improves significantly under four-day arrangements. Workers report reduced stress, better sleep, and more time for family, hobbies, and personal development. Mental health benefits are particularly notable, as additional rest prevents the burnout that plagues many modern workplaces.
paragraphs.zh: 在四天工作制下，员工福祉显著改善。工人报告压力减少、睡眠改善，有更多时间陪伴家人、发展爱好和个人成长。心理健康益处尤为显著，因为额外的休息可以防止困扰许多现代工作场所的职业倦怠。

paragraphs.en: Critics raise concerns about implementation challenges across different industries. Customer-facing roles and continuous operations may struggle to compress work into fewer days. Some worry that intensified daily work creates new pressures, potentially trading one form of stress for another.
paragraphs.zh: 批评者对不同行业的实施挑战提出了担忧。面向客户的角色和持续运营可能难以将工作压缩到更少的天数。一些人担心加强的日常工作会产生新的压力，可能只是用一种压力形式换另一种。

paragraphs.en: The conversation about working hours reflects broader questions about labor's role in modern life. Technology has dramatically increased productivity over decades, yet working hours have remained largely unchanged. Advocates argue that workers deserve to share in efficiency gains through reduced hours rather than solely through higher consumption.
paragraphs.zh: 关于工作时间的讨论反映了关于劳动在现代生活中角色的更广泛问题。几十年来，技术大大提高了生产力，但工作时间基本没有变化。倡导者认为，工人应该通过减少工时而非仅仅通过更高的消费来分享效率提升的成果。
